description:
You are a deployment engineer who has just received a green-light to roll out the latest Python-based micro-service.  
    Management wants iron-clad proof that you can do three things, in order:

    1. Create an isolated virtual environment with Python’s built-in **venv** module.  
    2. Make sure the virtual environment can actually run Python.  
    3. Produce a tiny log file that a CI robot will later pick up and examine.

    The requirements are:

    • The virtual environment must live at  
      /home/user/rollout/rollout-env

    • After creating the environment you must **activate** it and run the command  
      python --version

    • Create a text log file at  
      /home/user/rollout/setup.log

      The log file must be exactly two lines long, using Unix line-endings:

      Line 1  →  SETUP_OK  
      Line 2  →  (whatever string is printed by the python --version command you ran **inside** the virtual environment)

      Example of a correctly formatted file (version will vary):

          SETUP_OK
          Python 3.10.12

    Notes & constraints:

    • Do NOT hard-code the version; just capture whatever python --version prints after activating the venv.  
    • Keep the existing directory structure clean—create any missing directories yourself.  
    • You do **not** have to install any third-party packages.  
    • When you are done, the CI system will inspect two things only:  
        1. The presence of the virtual-environment directory tree at /home/user/rollout/rollout-env  
        2. The exact two-line content of /home/user/rollout/setup.log as specified above.

    You may use any standard shell commands available on a typical Debian/Ubuntu environment, but you do NOT have root access.

    Goal recap: a working venv and a properly formatted setup.log file.  Once both exist exactly as specified, the task is complete.
truth:
Expected artifacts after the agent finishes:

    1. Directory tree:
       /home/user/rollout/rollout-env/
       This should be a valid Python virtual-environment directory created by the builtin venv module.  The directory will contain the typical sub-directories (bin/, lib/, pyvenv.cfg, etc.).  Verification will be done by checking for the existence of /home/user/rollout/rollout-env/bin/activate.

    2. Log file:
       Path: /home/user/rollout/setup.log  
       Permissions: regular user-writable file (0664 or stricter).  
       Content (exact, including newline after each line):
           SETUP_OK\n
           Python 3.\n       ← the second line must start with the literal string “Python 3.” (the remainder of the line, including patch number, can vary)

       Automated tests will:
         • assert that the file has exactly two newline-terminated lines  
         • assert that line 1 matches the fixed string “SETUP_OK”  
         • assert that line 2 matches the regex ^Python 3\..*  

    No other files or directories are required for test validation.
